item.title: Version 1.1.13
item.description: Fixes a crash on the SEO page (the new peak-hours chart), restores click-to-open in the Articles list (a single click opens the article again), and resolves the SEO tab/filter clicks that were tied to the crash.

item.title: Version 1.1.12
item.description: The SEO peak-hours heatmap is now a native chart with proper axes, a smooth color scale, and hover/tap to read any hour's exact traffic. Prompt editing no longer auto-converts straight quotes and dashes into curly ones, so prompts stay exactly as you type them.

item.title: Version 1.1.11
item.description: Config is now a proper settings form: edit text values inline (no more pop-up dialog), and pick models, intervals, and schedule hours from native menus right in the row instead of a separate screen. Cleaner and faster to adjust.

item.title: Version 1.1.10
item.description: Prompt version history now shows a real diff: open any past version and a Diff/Raw toggle highlights exactly what changed vs. the current prompt — added/removed lines with a +/- gutter. Restoring an old version now asks for confirmation first, and the editor stops autocorrect from mangling prompts.

item.title: Version 1.1.9
item.description: Audit log gets real triage tools: filter by severity (with live error/warning counts), search across every field, and tap any event to expand its full message and pretty-printed metadata with copy. Faster to find what broke.

item.title: Version 1.1.8
item.description: Source Performance is now a real sortable table on Mac — click any column header to sort, with at-a-glance funnel bars and a new approval-rate column to spot which sources are worth keeping. Sources list gets shimmer loading too.

item.title: Version 1.1.7
item.description: Smoother live activity feed (decoded off the main thread), shimmer skeletons while pages load, sortable SEO performance tables, and Articles upgrades: keyboard triage (↩ open · ⌘↩ approve · ⌘⌫ deny, arrow keys to navigate) plus a comfortable reading column in the article detail.

item.title: Version 1.1.6
item.description: Reworked the Pipeline movement log as a native macOS table (Stage, Item, Movement, When, By) — stays smooth under load and is far more legible. Double-click or right-click a row to open it.

item.title: Version 1.1.5
item.description: Research rows: context menu replaces the redundant hover buttons. Pipeline movement feed reworked to update off the main thread for smoother scrolling under heavy load.

item.title: Version 1.1.4
item.description: Articles & Research: show row IDs and search by ID or title. Pipeline: clearer movement feed (shows From → To and the reason). Smaller featured image; fixed the verification-notes card stretching.

item.title: Version 1.1.3
item.description: Richer article right-click menu — Open Source, Copy Title, Copy Source URL; removed the redundant hover buttons (swipe and right-click already cover Approve/Deny).

item.title: Version 1.1.2
item.description: Smoother Pipeline movement feed; review actions no longer nudge the timestamp.

item.title: Version 1.1.1
item.description: Polish: more compact Top-to-publish cards, smoother and faster Pipeline & Articles feeds, and tidier pipeline movement rows.

item.title: Version 1.1.0
item.description: Major refresh: a redesigned editorial interface across every screen, plus a new Pipeline tab that tracks each article's journey end-to-end — a stage funnel, a live movement feed, and a per-item timeline. Also adds Ukrainian localization and dark-mode polish.

item.title: Version 1.0.15
item.description: Empty-state polish: 'No Research Topics', 'No system events', 'No digests yet', 'No Research Articles' placeholders now center properly in the viewport instead of pinning to the top-left corner. Same fix applied to the Body/Summary/Raw tabs on the Article reading page.

item.title: Version 1.0.14
item.description: Research now tracks the topic→article handoff: pending topics show a 'Queued #N' badge that opens the linked pipeline article, orphaned written-without-article rows surface a 'Missing article' warning, and a new filter chip bar mirrors the web (All / Ready / Queued / Needs article / Crawled / Dismissed).

item.title: Version 1.0.13
item.description: Stay-logged-in fix: 30-day tokens with silent refresh — you'll no longer see the login screen unless your server password actually changed. Adds Organization block toggle in Config and surfaces server-side updated_at sorting on article lists.

item.title: Version 1.0.12
item.description: Fixes auth on v1.0.11. The data-protection keychain (kSecUseDataProtectionKeychain=true) silently rejects SecItemAdd on ad-hoc-signed Mac bundles without a keychain-access-groups entitlement — so the login token never persisted and the login screen kept reappearing after correct credentials. Auth storage moves to UserDefaults: no keychain prompts, no missing-entitlement failures. For this app's threat model (small-team internal tool, HTTPS cookie auth, no secrets-at-rest requirement) the trade is correct. One re-login after the update and credentials persist normally.

item.title: Version 1.0.11
item.description: Stops the repeated keychain password prompts on macOS. Server URL moves to UserDefaults (no security value in keychain, and the legacy-keychain ACL was getting invalidated by every ad-hoc rebuild). Token + credentials switch to the data-protection keychain (kSecUseDataProtectionKeychain=true), which is bundle-ID-based rather than code-signature-based, so future ad-hoc rebuilds no longer trigger access prompts. One-time cost: this update will ask you to log in again, because credentials saved by v1.0.10 lived in the legacy keychain and don't migrate over. After re-login, you'll never see the prompt again.

item.title: Version 1.0.10
item.description: Adds the missing count badge on the Needs override filter chip. The count loader was iterating only the four original statuses and skipping the new approved_rejected one.

item.title: Version 1.0.9
item.description: Follow-up to v1.0.8 mirroring this week's news-pipe UI tweaks. Pending tab now hides articles that need override review — they have their own dedicated tab. The Needs override tab itself was renamed from Explorer rejected for accuracy. Override panel UX simplified: no more collapse, the textarea + Force generation button are visible whenever the explorer or writer blocked the article, and the textarea pre-populates with any prior editorial_override_note so a re-approval can build on previous reasoning. The regular Approve button is hidden (rather than just disabled) when override is required, matching the web. Override approve now sends the new redactor_notes field alongside note so the writer prompt receives the editor's guidance on the forced rewrite.

item.title: Version 1.0.8
item.description: Brings the app in line with two news-pipe UI features shipped this week. Articles list gains an Explorer rejected filter chip (alongside Pending / Approved / Published / Denied) — uses the new approved_rejected server filter to show pending_review articles the explorer flagged as not-publishable or needing manual review. The article reading page gains an editorial generation override flow: when the explorer or writer flagged an article, the normal Approve button is disabled and a warn-tinted override panel appears with a textarea for the editor's note. Tapping Approve for generation calls the new approve endpoint with override_generation=true so the note lands in the audit log alongside the approve event.

item.title: Version 1.0.7
item.description: Default server URL is now https://pipe.d1ops.net (the new NewsPipe domain). Existing installs that still pointed at the old IP migrate silently on first launch — anyone running a custom server keeps theirs untouched.

item.title: Version 1.0.6
item.description:
Brings the iOS/macOS app in line with the M1+M2 backend validation work.

  • Articles list shows a Review / Refused triage badge driven by the explorer's needs_manual_review and the writer's verification_status.
  • Article reading page gains an Explorer Validation panel: source / confidence / freshness / urgency chips, a 5-point key-facts checklist, primary-source link, verification notes.
  • New Writer Refusal banner surfaces the editorial note when the writer declined to generate a body.
  • Fixes a Sparkle update bug — earlier releases put the marketing string into sparkle:version instead of the build number, so newer marketing versions parsed as older than the running build and the update prompt never fired. From this release on, sparkle:version is the integer CFBundleVersion.

item.title: Version 1.0.5
item.description: Maintenance release. Internal release tooling moved to a single `make release` command — no user-facing changes.

item.title: Version 1.0.4
item.description:
SEO page polish.

  • Peak Hours heatmap — 7×24 grid (days × hours) replacing the old text list. Cell intensity scales with pageview count; tap a cell to see exact weekday / hour / views below.
  • 90-Day Trend tap-to-inspect — tap or drag along the chart to read the date and value at that point. The most recent point is selected by default.

This release also exercises the Sparkle auto-update flow over v1.0.3.

item.title: Version 1.0.3
item.description:
First actually-launchable build of the macOS app.

  • Fixed dyld rpath so Sparkle.framework resolves on macOS (the iOS-only @executable_path/Frameworks rpath couldn't find frameworks at the macOS bundle layout's Contents/Frameworks/).
  • Dropped hardened runtime from ad-hoc resign — incompatible with ad-hoc framework loading.
  • Sparkle auto-update channel established with this release.

v1.0.1 and v1.0.2 remain in GitHub release history but are no longer offered through this appcast.
